growth_records.cite: Taubner et al. Lipidomics and Comparative Metabolite Excretion Analysis of Methanogenic Archaea Reveal Organism-Specific Adaptations to Varying Temperatures and Substrate Concentrations. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01159-22. [PMC10134847]
growth_records.snippet: MER_mean 3.030 +/- 0.113 mmol L-1 h-1 ... WER_mean 6.852 +/- 0.180 ... Mean total AAER 9.617 +/- 0.916 umol L-1 h-1 ... M. okinawensis shows the highest rates of production of Glu (1.567 +/- 0.222), Val (1.265 +/- 0.202), Ile (1.152 +/- 0.1
growth_records.notes: Standard conditions (Table 1): 65C, 50 mL, 2x/day gassing. H2/CO2 uptake from stoichiometry 4H2+CO2->CH4+2H2O: qH2=4*3.030=12.12; qCO2=3.030 mmol/L/h. WER/MER=2.262. No mu reported (t_inc=78.6 h, OD_end=0.660+/-0.040). Medium is DSMZ 282 (modified); full base composition not given in text so only stated modifications listed; medium_composition_stated=false.

sec.method: reported highest MER +/- 0.55 at 65C, 25 mL, 2x/day gassing

growth_records.cite: Taubner et al. Lipidomics and Comparative Metabolite Excretion Analysis of Methanogenic Archaea Reveal Organism-Specific Adaptations to Varying Temperatures and Substrate Concentrations. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01159-22. [PMC10134847]
growth_records.snippet: The highest MERs for M. okinawensis and M. villosus were observed at their optimal growth temperatures (65C and 80C, respectively) at 25 mL with twice-per-day gassing (6.24 +/- 0.55 mmol L-1 h-1 for M. okinawensis and 10.23 +/- 0.79 mmol L-
growth_records.notes: Non-standard high-MER condition: 65C, 25 mL liquid, 2x/day gassing. MER=6.24 mmol/L/h reported. H2/CO2 uptake from stoichiometry: qH2=4*6.24=24.96; qCO2=6.24 mmol/L/h. No mu or medium composition detail beyond DSMZ 282 (modified).
